elements.sent-2411-001: Unmanned aerial vehicles (UAVs) are the future of aviation.
elements.sent-2411-002: FAA forecasts that there will be 1.81 million units of drone fleet by 2026~\cite{FAA .}
elements.sent-2411-003: With so many UAVs flying in the airspace, tracking and predicting the motion of UAVs become important.
elements.sent-2411-004: The security service needs to predict misused drones in public areas including airports, mass events, or public demonstrations~\cite{laurenzis2020prediction .}
elements.sent-2411-005: Meanwhile, in-network UAVs need to predict the trajectory of out-network UAVs to avoid them, which is especially important in UAV landing management due to the congested airspace near vertiports~\cite{zhou2020optimized .}
elements.sent-2411-006: Conflict detection and resolution(CD\&R) technology, plays an important role in the air traffic system~\cite{lin2018uav,wu2022safety and highly relies on accurate and timely predictions of out-network UAV trajectories~\cite{kang2020model .}}
elements.sent-2411-007: There are many methods have been proposed to predict the trajectory of UAVs.
elements.sent-2411-008: However, existing methods for UAV trajectory prediction are ordinarily very slow and unable to be applied online~\cite{xie2022efficient .}
elements.sent-2411-009: In addition to fast prediction, higher accuracy of prediction results is required.
elements.sent-2411-010: Many planning methods are bothered by the uncertainty of the future prediction.
elements.sent-2411-011: Aircraft trajectory prediction is always a popular research topic and there are many methods proposed.
elements.sent-2411-012: The Recurrent Neural network is proposed to predict aircraft trajectory with weather features\cite{pang2019recurrent .}
elements.sent-2411-013: The Hidden Markov Model (HMM) is recently applied to predict trajectories considering environmental uncertainties\cite{ayhan2016aircraft .}
elements.sent-2411-014: The deep generative convolutional recurrent neural network (RNN) approach is applied for 4D trajectory prediction\cite{liu2018predicting .}
elements.sent-2411-015: A hybrid 3-dimensional UAV modeling framework, which integrates the physics-based and data-based models, can predict the motion of UAVs using only a small amount of real fight data\cite{wang20193 .}
elements.sent-2411-016: Some aircraft prediction methods focus on modeling aircraft trajectories around the terminal.
elements.sent-2411-017: One traditional way is to predict the aircraft near the terminal by the Physical equation of motion\cite{chatterji1999short .}
elements.sent-2411-018: The bayesian network can learn statistical representations of dynamic variables of aircraft and predict the motion\cite{kochenderfer2010airspace .}
elements.sent-2411-019: Probabilistic generative model\cite{barratt2018learning predicts the aircraft trajectory by learning from position measurements.}
elements.sent-2411-020: The neural network generator has been proven to predict many objects such as language, image, and trajectories of pedestrians.
elements.sent-2411-021: Transformer~\cite{vaswani2017attention is the state-of-the-art method to draw global dependencies between input and output.}
elements.sent-2411-022: BERT~\cite{devlin2018bert , uses Transformer as one of the encoders and is able to understand and process natural language.}
elements.sent-2411-023: GATs~\cite{velivckovic2017graph achieved the state-of-the-art result across four established transductive and inductive graph benchmarks leveraging masked self-attentional layers.}
elements.sent-2411-024: At the same time, the transformer has successfully been used with GANs.
elements.sent-2411-025: SAGAN~\cite{zhang2019self achieved pioneering image generation results by using a transformer layer in GANs.}
elements.sent-2411-026: ViTGAN~\cite{lee2021vitgan achieves a comparable performance of image generation to state-of-the-art CNN-based GANs without using convolution or pooling.}
elements.sent-2411-027: CNN also performs decently recognizing image and text~\cite{long2015fully,ren2015faster \cite{he2016deep,huang2017densely .}}
elements.sent-2411-028: GANs have proven to be able to capture the movement of mobile objects and predict their trajectories in many existing studies.
elements.sent-2411-029: For example, Social-GAN~\cite{gupta2018social is able to predict pedestrians' movement considering the social interaction between pedestrians.}
elements.sent-2411-030: SoPhie~\cite{sadeghian2019sophie can understand the scene context besides social behavior and generate pedestrian trajectories.}
elements.sent-2411-031: CGAN was proposed in~\cite{pang2020conditional to solve weather-related aircraft trajectory prediction problems.}
elements.sent-2411-032: Therefore it is rational to train a Neural network to predict the landing trajectory of the UAS.
elements.sent-2411-033: The paper proposes to predict the landing trajectory for UAS with a GAN-based method.
elements.sent-2411-034: The major contributions of this paper are summarized as follows:.
elements.sent-2411-035: This paper adopts a 2D trajectory GAN-based method to predict 3D landing trajectories of UAS.
elements.sent-2411-036: The proposed method can generate prediction very fast and outperform the Gaussian Mixture Regression(GMR) in accuracy.
elements.sent-2411-037: This paper provides a real UAS landing trajectory dataset for future research.
elements.sent-2411-038: After GANs are well-trained, the discriminator part can be used to evaluate other predictions.
elements.sent-2411-039: The paper's structure is as follows.
elements.sent-2411-040: Section~\ref{sec:background explains the background of GANs and GMM; Section \ref{sec: method introduces the trajectory, how the dataset is created, and the architecture of the generator; Section~\ref{sec: results shows the results by comparing the GANs and GMM; Section~\ref{sec: conc concludes this paper.}}}}
elements.sent-2411-041: Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM)~\cite{hochreiter1997long is a novel recurrent network architecture in conjunction with an appropriate gradient-based learning algorithm.}
elements.sent-2411-042: The LSTM can enforce constant error flow so it can accelerate learning store information over extended time intervals.
elements.sent-2411-043: LSTM consists of multiplicative gate units that can learn to open or close the access to the error flow.
elements.sent-2411-044: LSTM can be used as the encoder and the decoder in GANs.
elements.sent-2411-045: A Generative Adversarial Network consists of two neural networks, generator and discriminator, trained in opposition to each other~\cite{goodfellow2014generative .}
elements.sent-2411-046: The training procedure is similar to a two-player min-max game with the following objective function: \begin{equation}\min\limits_{G} \max\limits_{D} V(D,G) = \mathbb{E}_{x\sim p_{data}(x)}[\log{D(x)}] + \mathbb{E}_{z\sim p_{z}(z)}[\log{1-D(G(z))}]\end{equation} In this paper, G denotes the generator that inputs factors and generates trajectory.
elements.sent-2411-047: Generator G takes the input factors and output trajectory G(z).
elements.sent-2411-048: D denotes discriminator and D(x) output the probability that x is a trajectory is desired.
elements.sent-2411-049: Gupta proposed social-GAN~\cite{gupta2018social that can understand human motion behavior.}
elements.sent-2411-050: The social-GAN is able to observe the motion trajectories of pedestrians and predict the trajectories of pedestrians.
elements.sent-2411-051: Social-GAN combines tools from sequence prediction and generative adversarial networks.
elements.sent-2411-052: The generator of the social-GAN is consists of an LSTM-based encoder, a pooling module, and an LSTM-based decoder.
elements.sent-2411-053: The LSTM encoder encodes the motion trajectories to hidden items $h$.
elements.sent-2411-054: Then the pooling module pool the hidden items $h$ while combining desired information $i$ and the hidden items $h$ together.
elements.sent-2411-055: Finally, the decoder decodes the hidden items and outputs the predicted trajectory.
elements.sent-2411-056: In this paper, Gaussian Mixture Regression(GMR) predictor is the baseline method.
elements.sent-2411-057: GMR predictor was proposed in \cite{calinon2007learning and the GMR predictor we use in this paper is proposed in\cite{fabisch2021gmr .}}
elements.sent-2411-058: The GMR predictor is one of the most popular predictors.
elements.sent-2411-059: The training process of GMR is shown as equation~(\ref{gmrt ). \begin{equation}\label{gmrt} p(\boldsymbol{x}, \boldsymbol{y})=\sum_{k=1}^K \pi_k \mathcal{N}_k\left(\boldsymbol{x}, \boldsymbol{y} \mid \boldsymbol{\mu}_{\boldsymbol{x} \boldsymbol{y}_k}, \boldsymbol{\Sigma}_{\boldsymbol{x} \boldsymbol{y}_k}\right)\end{equation} where $\mathcal{N _k\left(\boldsymbol{x , \boldsymbol{y \mid \boldsymbol{\mu _{\boldsymbol{x \boldsymbol{y _k , \boldsymbol{\Sigma _{\boldsymbol{x \boldsymbol{y _k}\right)$ are Gaussian distributions with mean $\boldsymbol{\mu _{\boldsymbol{x \boldsymbol{y _k}$ and covariance $\boldsymbol{\Sigma _{\boldsymbol{x}\boldsymbol{y _k}$, $K$ is the number of Gaussians, and $\pi_k \in [0, 1]$ are priors that sum up to one.}}}}}}}}}}}}}}}}
elements.formula-2411-002: \begin{equation}\label{gmrt} p(x, y)=\sum_{k=1}^K \pi_k N_k\left(x, y \mid \mu_{x y_k}, \Sigma_{x y_k}\right)\end{equation}
elements.sent-2411-060: After the GMR is trained, GMR predicts distributions of variables $\boldsymbol{y}$ by computing the conditional distribution $p(\boldsymbol{y \mid \boldsymbol{x )$.}}
elements.sent-2411-061: The conditional distribution of each individual Gaussian \begin{equation}\mathcal{N}\left(\boldsymbol{x}, \boldsymbol{y} \mid \boldsymbol{\mu}_{x y}, \boldsymbol{\Sigma}_{x y}\right) \boldsymbol{\mu}_{\boldsymbol{x} y}=\left(\begin{array}{c} \mu_x \mu_y \end{array}\right), \quad \boldsymbol{\Sigma}_{x y}=\left(\begin{array}{cc} \boldsymbol{\Sigma}_{x \boldsymbol{x}} \boldsymbol{\Sigma}_{\boldsymbol{x} y} \boldsymbol{\Sigma}_{y x} \boldsymbol{\Sigma}_{y y} \end{array}\right)\end{equation} is defined by \begin{equation}\mu_{y \mid x}=\mu_y+\Sigma_{\boldsymbol{y} x} \Sigma_{x \boldsymbol{x}}^{-1}\left(x-\mu_x\right) \\\end{equation} \begin{equation}\Sigma_{y \mid x}=\Sigma_{y y}-\Sigma_{y x} \Sigma_{x \boldsymbol{x}}^{-1} \Sigma_{x y}\end{equation} the conditional distribution of each individual Gaussian and their priors is computed by equation \begin{equation}\pi_{\boldsymbol{y} \mid \boldsymbol{x}_k}=\frac{\mathcal{N}_k\left(\boldsymbol{x} \mid \boldsymbol{\mu}_{\boldsymbol{x} k}, \boldsymbol{\Sigma}_{\boldsymbol{x} k}\right)}{\sum_{l=1}^K \mathcal{N}_l\left(\boldsymbol{x} \mid \boldsymbol{\mu}_{\boldsymbol{x} l}, \boldsymbol{\Sigma}_{\boldsymbol{x} l}\right)}\end{equation} to obtain the conditional distribution \begin{equation}p(\boldsymbol{y} \mid \boldsymbol{x})=\sum_{k=1}^K \pi_{\boldsymbol{y} \mid \boldsymbol{x}_k} \mathcal{N}_k\left(\boldsymbol{y} \mid \boldsymbol{\mu}_{\boldsymbol{y} \mid \boldsymbol{x}_k}, \boldsymbol{\Sigma}_{\boldsymbol{y} \mid \boldsymbol{x}_k}\right)\end{equation}.
elements.formula-2411-003: \begin{equation}N\left(x, y \mid \mu_{x y}, \Sigma_{x y}\right) \mu_{x y}=\left({c} \mu_x \mu_y \right), \quad \Sigma_{x y}=\left({cc} \Sigma_{x x} \Sigma_{x y} \Sigma_{y x} \Sigma_{y y} \right)\end{equation}
elements.formula-2411-006: \begin{equation}\pi_{y \mid x_k}=\frac{N_k\left(x \mid \mu_{x k}, \Sigma_{x k}\right)}{\sum_{l=1}^K N_l\left(x \mid \mu_{x l}, \Sigma_{x l}\right)}\end{equation}
elements.sent-2411-062: Mathematically, Let T denotes the trajectory sequence of consists of multiple flight statuses.
elements.sent-2411-063: Let $x_{t+1 \in R^n$ the flight status at $t + 1$, will be determined by the previous status, dynamic model, and control input according to ordinary differential equation (ODE): \begin{equation}T_{t_1, t_2} = [x_{t_1}, x_{t_1+1}, ...x_{t_2 - 1} ,x_{t_2}]\end{equation} \begin{equation}x_{t+1} = f(x_{t}, a_t(E)), a_t \in \mathbb{A}\end{equation} where $f$ denotes flight the dynamic model.}
elements.sent-2411-064: Let $a(E)$ called action function in these paper.
elements.sent-2411-065: However, in real world, flight dynamic model has noises and high computational cost.
elements.sent-2411-066: On the other hands, environment factors and previous status are only variables required to derive a trajectory.
elements.sent-2411-067: In this paper, the only flight status we focus on is position.
elements.sent-2411-068: In order to predict the future trajectory without flight dynamic model and action function, the generator model(G) was proposed: \begin{equation}T_{t_1, t_2} = G(T_{t_0,t_1})\end{equation} where the Generator takes the previous trajectory sequence as input and outputs a predicted trajectory sequence.
elements.sent-2411-069: In this paper, all the previous trajectory sequence contains 10 points, and all the predicted trajectory sequence contains 10 points as well.
elements.sent-2411-070: In this paper, we prepare three different types of datasets to train the proposed model and test the learning capacity.
elements.sent-2411-071: We will start with a sample dataset as shown in Figure~\ref{vertical to test how the generator works.}
elements.sent-2411-072: The dataset includes the trajectories starting from four different directions to the destination (0,0,0).
elements.sent-2411-073: The average initial position is (-200, 200, 75) for direction1, (200, -200, 75) for direction2, (-200, -200, 75) for direction3, and (200, 200, 75) for direction4.
elements.sent-2411-074: The variance for the x, and y positions is 50, and for the z position is 3.5.
elements.sent-2411-075: The drone first flies horizontally to the points right above the destination, then land vertically.
elements.sent-2411-076: The second dataset (as shown in Figure~\ref{linear ) we used to evaluate this method is simulated linear landing data.}
elements.sent-2411-077: Same as vertical data, the drone flies from four different initial positions and land at position (0,0,0).
elements.sent-2411-078: The average initial position is (-400, 400, 140) for direction1, (400, -400, 140) for direction2, (-400, -400, 140) for direction3, and (400, 400, 140) for direction4.
elements.sent-2411-079: The variance for the x, and y positions is 100, and for the z position is 7.5.
elements.sent-2411-080: In contrast to vertical landing, the drone flight flies straight forward to the destination.
elements.sent-2411-081: Real trajectories as shown in Figure~\ref{real will be collected by the real pilot.}
elements.sent-2411-082: Similar to the simulated data, the real pilot flies the drone from different initial positions and land at the destination.
elements.sent-2411-083: The initial position and trajectory pattern will fully rely on how the real pilot wants to control the drone.
elements.sent-2411-084: In this paper, the drone, as shown in Figure~\ref{drone , is a customized DJI Tello, which we can track and localize by the motion capture system.}
elements.sent-2411-085: The trajectory can be treated as a kind of data sequence, therefore, LSTM, which is one of the best neural network structures to understand data sequences, can be used to understand and generate the trajectory.
elements.sent-2411-086: Social-GANs\cite{gupta2018social was proposed to predict pedestrians' future trajectory based on their past trajectory and social interaction.}
elements.sent-2411-087: Similar to social-GANs, We show one of the trajectories that are desired by the LSTM and tell LSTM some factors, then the LSTM generates trajectories based on the trajectory we showed to it.
elements.sent-2411-088: The sequence data are needed to be embedded into a fixed-length vector before sending it into the LSTM cell.
elements.sent-2411-089: The LSTM cell will encode the embedded vector and previous hidden item into a current hidden item. \begin{equation}e_t = \sigma(x_t) h^{el}_t = LSTM_{encoder}(h^{el}_{t-1}, e_t; W_{encoder})\end{equation} where $e_t$ denotes the embedding vector.
elements.sent-2411-091: The pooling will input the environment factors and combine them into hidden items come from the LSTM encoder layer: \begin{equation}p = P(E, z)\end{equation} \begin{equation}e_t = \phi(x_{t-1};W_{em})\end{equation} The the LSTM decoder layer will take the combined hidden item, generate the trajectory. \begin{equation}h^{dl}_{t} = LSTM_{decoder}(\gamma(h^{dl}_{t-1},p), e_t;W_{decoder})\end{equation} \begin{equation}x_t = \gamma(h^{dl}_t)\end{equation}.
elements.sent-2411-092: Instead of a traditional mathematical model, we use a neural network as a discriminator to evaluate the generated trajectory.
elements.sent-2411-093: Based on the author's knowledge, there is no traditional mathematical model that is completely able to identify if a trajectory is a good trajectory.
elements.sent-2411-094: The discriminator inputs the trajectory T and gives a score s.
elements.sent-2411-095: If the given data is real, the discriminator is expected to give a high score.
elements.sent-2411-096: The score s is the main component of the loss function of the generator.
elements.sent-2411-097: In this paper, the discriminator is an LSTM network. \begin{equation}s = LSTM(T)\end{equation}.
elements.sent-2411-098: The proposed GANs will be tested in three prepared datasets introduced in section~\ref{data .}
elements.sent-2411-099: For each dataset, there will be 3000(2430 for real) trajectories for training and 100 trajectories for evaluation.
elements.sent-2411-100: Figure~\ref{GMR ,\ref{GANs shows examples of the GMR and GAN predicting the landing trajectory.}}
elements.sent-2411-101: The accuracy of the prediction will be evaluated by the Average Displacement Error(ade) between the predicted trajectory and the real trajectory.
elements.sent-2411-102: Then, we will test if the discriminator can distinguish good and bad predictions.
elements.sent-2411-103: The equation to calculate ade for one point is shown as equation~\ref{ade \begin{equation}\label{ade} ade = mean(\sqrt{(dx)^2 + (dy)^2 + (dz)^2})\end{equation} where dx is the displacement between the predicted point and the ground truth point in the x-axis, dy is the displacement in the y-axis and dz is the displacement in the z-axis.}
elements.sent-2411-104: The trained GAN and GMR observe 100 evaluation trajectories and predict possible trajectories.
elements.sent-2411-105: The ade is the mean of 100 displacement errors between prediction and ground truth.
elements.sent-2411-106: The ade result for each point and the dataset is shown as tabel~\ref{tab: ade .}
elements.sent-2411-107: The results show that GAN has a very good performance to predict the first four points, while the predicts get worse as the trajectory gets longer.
elements.sent-2411-108: For the vertical and linear datasets, GAN only outperforms GMR for the first four points.
elements.sent-2411-109: However, for the real dataset, GAN outperforms GMR for all the points.
elements.sent-2411-110: Because the vertical and linear dataset is simulated and has a smaller variance, it is easier for GMR, which is a Gaussian-based method, to predict.
elements.sent-2411-111: In contrast, in the real dataset, real pilots control the drone more randomly and the control logic is more subtle, GAN is able to mine the hidden rules and gets better results.
elements.sent-2411-112: Figure~\ref{displacement shows a comparison of the prediction displacement in each axis for the real dataset between GAN and GMR.}
elements.sent-2411-113: The displacement of GMR predictions obviously larger than GAN in all axes.
elements.sent-2411-114: In conclusion, GAN has much better accuracy in predicting the trajectories from the real dataset, while performing decently in predicting the trajectories from the simulated dataset.
elements.sent-2411-115: However, the longer the predicting is, the worse the GAN will perform.
elements.sent-2411-116: The discriminator of the GANs can evaluate a trajectory and give a score.
elements.sent-2411-117: We show the discriminator 100 true trajectories and 100 fake trajectories from each dataset and let the discriminator generates the scores for those trajectories.
elements.sent-2411-118: The results are shown as table~\ref{tab: score .}
elements.sent-2411-119: According to the result, the discriminator gives higher scores to the true trajectories.
elements.sent-2411-120: It proves that the discriminator can distinguish true and fake trajectories.
elements.figure-2411-010: \caption{\label{tab: score} score given to real and fake trajectories by the discriminator} \begin{tabular}{c c c c c c c} \multicolumn{2}{c}{vertical} \multicolumn{2}{c}{linear} \multicolumn{2}{c}{real} method True Fake True Fake True Fake score 2.79$\pm$0.31 -9.7$\pm$0.43 2.99$\pm$0.77 -7.93$\pm$0.67 2.32$\pm$2.76 0.21$\pm$0.86 \end{tabular}
elements.sent-2411-121: In this paper, the LSTM-GAN-based method can accurately predict the motion of UAVs during the landing.
elements.sent-2411-122: The prediction error is lower than the baseline method.
elements.sent-2411-123: Meanwhile, the discriminator can give a high score to ground truth trajectories while giving a low score to fake trajectories.
elements.sent-2411-124: It proves that the trained discriminator can be used to evaluate the prediction.
elements.sent-2411-125: A dataset that includes a large number of trajectories of UAVs controlled by real pilots is also created.
elements.sent-2411-126: However, according to the results, the accuracy of prediction results becomes lower as the prediction length is longer.
elements.sent-2411-127: This method may not work for long-term prediction.
elements.sent-2411-128: In the future, a new neural network encoder and decoder, which can process a larger amount of information is required for this GANs framework.. \bibliographystyle{aiaa.}
